prompt: Explain what a "closure" is in programming, in exactly one sentence. The sentence can be long.
response: In programming, a "closure" is a record storing a function together with an environment: a mapping associating each free variable of the function (variables that are used locally, but defined in an enclosing scope) with the value or reference to which the variable is bound, enabling the function to access those captured variables even after the scope in which they were defined has finished executing.
